description_long: This object is a heavy-duty anvil hammer, featuring a robust black metal anvil head securely mounted atop a thick, rectangular wooden block. The anvil head is solid, with a flat striking surface and a pointed horn, showing signs of wear and metallic patina. The wooden base is reinforced with three black metal bands, tightly wrapped around its circumference for added stability and durability. A thin black cord or strap is attached to the side, possibly for securing or carrying the hammer. The wood displays a natural grain and some surface irregularities, indicating frequent use. The overall construction suggests it is designed for blacksmithing or heavy-duty metalwork in an urban workshop or garage setting.
description: A black metal anvil hammer mounted on a thick, reinforced wooden block with black metal bands and a side strap, showing signs of wear and heavy use.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full width of the anvil head, the wooden block base, and the three black metal bands securing the wood.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the narrow profile of the anvil head, the thickness of the wooden block, and the black cord or strap hanging down.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the anvil head, the wooden block, and the metal bands, with the horn of the anvil visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the other narrow profile, the wooden block, and the black bands, with the strap visible on the left.
attribute_handle_type: Rectangular wooden block base, straight, untreated, with visible wood grain and reinforced by black metal bands; not ergonomic or textured for grip.
attribute_head_type: Solid blacksmith anvil head with a flat striking face and a pointed horn; no claw or peen, designed for heavy striking and shaping metal.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible wear, scratches, and patina on the anvil head; wood shows surface irregularities and signs of use.
attribute_attachment_or_accessory: Black cord or strap attached to the side of the wooden base.
